name: SPI Bus
description: SPI is a very common high-speed protocol for a lot of devices. The ESPHome SPI component implements only the host controller role, where it controls the bus, and writes or reads data from peripherals attached to the bus.

config_entries.label: Type
config_entries.description: Choose between single for standard 1 bit bus SPI (the default), quad and octal.

config_entries.label: Clk Pin
config_entries.description: The pin used for the clock line of the SPI bus.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Data Pins
config_entries.description: Must be a list of exactly 4 pins to be used for the quad SPI output data lines, or exactly 8 pins for octal mode. Not used for single mode.

config_entries.label: Mode

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for this SPI hub if you need multiple SPI hubs.

config_entries.label: Interface
config_entries.description: Controls which hardware or software SPI implementation should be used.
